directory_structure_summary: Monorepo with 9 packages under packages/: core (types, parsers, linter, runtime), engine (Puppeteer+FFmpeg capture), producer (full render pipeline), cli (user-facing CLI with citty), studio (React browser editor), player (web component), shader-transitions (WebGL shaders), aws-lambda (Lambda SDK), gcp-cloud-run (Cloud Run deployment). Root also contains docs/ (Mintlify documentation), registry/ (catalog of blocks/components/examples), skills/ (AI agent skill definitions), scripts/ (build/release tooling), examples/ (deployment examples), releases/ (changelogs). No root tsconfig.json; each package has its own tsconfig.
architecture_pattern: Modular monorepo with layered rendering pipeline: HTML composition → Core (parse/lint/compile) → Engine (Puppeteer frame capture) → Producer (encode + audio mix) → output MP4. Studio and CLI are front-ends consuming this pipeline. Adapter-based animation: HTML pages reference animation libraries (GSAP, CSS, WAAPI, Lottie, etc.) via script tags, and the engine seeks frames through a deterministic frame-adapter protocol. Runtime is a browser IIFE that manages composition lifecycle, frame seeking, and animation timeline control.
notes: HyperFrames is an HTML-to-video rendering framework by HeyGen, analogous to Remotion but using plain HTML instead of React for composition authoring. It is agent-first (AI coding agents write HTML compositions) and deterministic (same input produces identical video output). Published under Apache 2.0 license. Uses Git LFS for golden regression test baselines (~240MB of MP4 files). CI includes build, lint, format, typecheck, test, fallow audit, studio smoke test, CLI smoke test (both monorepo source and packed global install), file size checks, and semantic PR title validation. Requires FFmpeg as a system dependency. ONNX runtime enables AI-powered background removal in CLI. The producer package includes extensive regression/parity/perf harnesses comparing preview output to final render output.
